title: Repair company collection, review, map, account deletion, mobile scrolling, and commercial
description: Complete the reported tenant SaaS defects: approved company records on collector map and capture totals; evidence-rich company review; tenant-only field/profile UX; worldwide user-centered map; safe self-service erasure and removal of test accounts; mobile console scrolling/responsiveness; complete UX/accessibility/performance validation; deliver 15-second 1080x1920 commercial.
design: Preserve the shared ADL engine while isolating company context. Keep operational history under random tombstones. Apply ADL light, map-native, daylight-readable design with 48px controls and EN/FR copy.
acceptance_criteria: Company users see only tenant branding/forms/data and role-appropriate screens; approved tenant captures appear on the correct vertical map and user totals; reviewers inspect photos/GPS/device/form metadata before decisions; account deletion requires deliberate confirmation and preserves anonymous operational history; test accounts are erased without recreation; mobile pages scroll; tests/build/E2E pass; vertical commercial is delivered; release is pushed.
close_reason: Completed tenant workflows, privacy erasure, responsive UX, audit, test suite, and verified 15-second commercial.

title: Repair company collection, review, map, account deletion, and mobile scrolling
description: Production defects: company submissions do not appear on collector map or collector metrics after approval; company review lacks photo/evidence/metadata forensics; company console does not scroll reliably on phones; implement guarded self-service privacy erasure and erase test@example.com and test1234@test.com without destroying historical records.
acceptance_criteria: Approved company records appear on the correct tenant/project collector map; collector submission count reflects their captures; review exposes all photos, form data, GPS/time/evidence metadata and risk context before approve/reject; long public/company screens scroll on phone; self-delete requires authenticated user, exact typed confirmation, acknowledgement, password reauth where available, last-owner/admin guards, session revocation and anonymization; named production test identities and bootstrap credentials are removed; automated tests and production verification pass.
close_reason: Completed company map/metrics, evidence forensics, mobile scrolling, guarded erasure, and production account anonymization.

title: Add guarded self-service account deletion and remove test accounts
description: Delete production test@example.com and test1234@test.com safely after resolving all FK/data ownership effects. Add authenticated self-service account deletion for public ADL and company-console users with strong accidental-deletion safeguards, server-side authorization, ownership constraints, auditability, session invalidation, bilingual responsive UI, and repair phone vertical scrolling so users can reach all profile/settings controls.
design: Use a server-side transactional deletion service. Require recent password confirmation for credential accounts plus an exact typed confirmation phrase and a second explicit confirmation step. Block deletion when the user is the last owner of a company until ownership is transferred or the company is suspended/closed by an ADL admin. Preserve or anonymize contribution/tenant records according to referential and audit requirements, delete credentials/profile/memberships/sessions, and sign out immediately.
acceptance_criteria: The two named production test accounts are absent. Any authenticated public or company user can reach and initiate deletion from Profile/console Settings on desktop and phone. All relevant phone surfaces scroll vertically with fixed navigation respected. Deletion cannot be triggered by GET, CSRF, another user ID, or a single accidental click; it requires password reauthentication when applicable, exact typed confirmation, and a final explicit action. Last company owners are blocked with actionable transfer guidance. Successful deletion invalidates sessions, removes login credentials and PII, preserves required contribution/company data without exposing the deleted identity, writes a non-PII audit event, and passes unit/mobile-browser/security/build checks.
close_reason: Completed guarded self-service deletion, last-owner/admin protection, transactional anonymization, production test-account erasure, and mobile access.

title: Restrict console organization owners to ADL admin provisioning
description: Organization creation currently lets a normal authenticated account create a console workspace and become owner. Enforce that only ADL admins can create organizations/initial owners; non-admin accounts with no membership must be directed to accept an invite rather than onboarding.
design: Authorize with the canonical session isAdmin/role before createOrganization. Treat owner as an ADL-admin-provisioned role and keep invitation roles owner-excluded.
acceptance_criteria: platform_org_create returns 403 for authenticated non-admin users before any store write; ADL admins can create organizations; console onboarding/create-workspace UI is unavailable to non-admins; invited users can still join existing organizations; tests and CI pass.
notes: Implemented server authorization for organization creation and owner promotion; tenant-scoped role workspaces (viewer approved data, reviewer approval queue, manager operations, owner administration); non-admin no-org invitation state; API/store/client/UI/unit/E2E coverage. Full npm run test:ci and 5-role Playwright smoke tests pass.
close_reason: Completed in 0db86bc: ADL-admin-only organization/owner creation, role-scoped tenant console workspaces, approved-data viewer, reviewer queue, and full unit/E2E coverage.

title: Preserve organization context through collector invite signup
description: A collector opening an email invitation must join the inviting organization and inherit its company/project vertical scope. The flow must not send them through generic seven-vertical ADL account onboarding.
acceptance_criteria: Invite link survives authentication/account creation; invited email is enforced; account is provisioned into the inviting organization with collector role and organization verticals; generic onboarding is bypassed; end-to-end tests cover a new user.
close_reason: Invite token now persists safely through signup/sign-in, bypasses generic onboarding, resumes acceptance automatically, enforces invited email, and lands in the inviting organization projects.

title: Fix console invite account binding and logout
description: Production collaborator invite links auto-accept for whichever authenticated browser session opens them, then navigate to Projects. handleInviteAccept checks only authentication, not invite.email against session identity, and upsertMemberRole can overwrite an existing owner's role with the invited role. ConsoleShell also exposes no sign-out action, preventing account switching.
design: Bind invite acceptance server-side to the JWT email before any membership mutation. Reject already-member acceptance. Propagate stable API error codes to JoinScreen. Wire existing auth signOut client into ConsoleApp/ConsoleShell with accessible UI.
acceptance_criteria: Invite acceptance rejects a signed-in account whose normalized email differs from the invite email; existing organization membership cannot be downgraded through invite acceptance; mismatch UI explains the account issue and offers sign-out; console shell exposes bilingual sign-out with pending/error state; focused tests, build, CI, and live deployment pass.
close_reason: Fixed, tested, deployed, and verified live: production console uses hashed assets, invite mismatch recovery and sign-out are present, manifest is valid.

title: Fix production /console routing to built console entry

User also reported Vite localhost asset errors from a prior/stale response. Add explicit extensionless console routing ahead of the fallback and regression coverage for route/build output.
design: Keep Vite multi-page build. Add explicit Vercel rewrites before the catch-all and a lightweight config/build regression test using existing Node test patterns.
acceptance_criteria: /console and /console/ resolve to built console.html; response has compiled /assets/console-*.js and no /index.tsx, @vite/client, @react-refresh, localhost, or landscape-block; npm build passes; routing regression check passes.
close_reason: Fixed and deployed: explicit Vercel rewrites serve console.html for /console and /console/; regression test and production build pass; live responses use compiled console asset with no dev URLs or portrait blocker; manifest is valid JSON.

Add an integration test that runs these queries against a real Postgres (or a parse/prepare check) to catch SQL type errors. See PR #58.

title: Expose classic ADL points and freshness to company collectors
description: Company Explore currently replaces public ADL points with approved tenant records. Collectors therefore cannot open an ordinary existing ADL point, see the classic freshness/last-updated/missing-fields detail, or start company enrichment from that point. Load the project-scoped nearby point projection in company mode, render those points alongside private tenant records, preserve the classic Details experience, and route enrichment into the selected company form.
design: Treat platform_point_nearby as the privacy-safe read projection. Add UI-only target metadata to DataPoint so classic Details can stay shared while App routes the CTA to PlatformCollectionFlow. Merge/dedupe nearby ADL points with tenant records by pointId without exposing records across organizations.
acceptance_criteria: With a company collector and geolocation, nearby ADL points returned by platform_point_nearby appear on map/list; tapping one opens classic point Details with last update, freshness, trust and missing fields; the CTA says Update/Enrich (not sign in) and opens PlatformCollectionFlow with the active project/record type and point pre-attached; tenant approved records remain private and visually distinguishable; GPS/distance/cooldown server gates remain unchanged; EN/FR mobile E2E and quality gates pass.
close_reason: Company collectors now load privacy-safe nearby ADL points beside tenant records, open classic freshness/trust/gap details, and launch an explicit bilingual Update/Enrich CTA into the pre-attached company form. Marker overlap, GPS permission/retry, dedupe, and public privacy are covered; 683 unit tests, 3 mobile collector smokes, lint, typecheck, and build pass.

title: Company collector cannot update linked point from map

Make a company map point directly actionable, preselect the matching company form and linked point, require fresh capture GPS, and cover the mobile journey.
design: Reuse PlatformCollectionFlow with an explicit initial target rather than duplicating a form. Treat record.evidence.gps only as point display coordinates; never as fresh capture GPS for distance validation.
acceptance_criteria: A collector can tap a linked company point on the map, see an Update this point action, enter PlatformCollectionFlow with the matching organization/project/record type and point pre-attached, capture fresh GPS, and submit pointId; unlinked standalone company records remain read-only; EN/FR and 48px touch targets preserved; targeted tests and build pass.
close_reason: Fixed company point tap/update path: 48px marker remains above collector location, linked company detail exposes bilingual update CTA, company form is preselected/pre-attached, fresh GPS is mandatory, and mobile E2E verifies pointId submission. 683 unit tests, 3 collector smokes, typecheck, lint, and build pass.

Unit, browser, migration, lint, typecheck and build gates pass.

title: Make native copy bilingual and iOS typography scalable
description: The iOS app shows English location states, Apple Maps, mission/badge text, and Earned while French is selected. Android sync status is also English-only. iOS ADLFont uses fixed Font.custom sizes without relativeTo, and dense screens use 9-12pt labels with line limits, so Dynamic Type is not reliably supported.
design: Move runtime strings into one EN/FR localization layer on both platforms. Make LocationProvider expose state rather than localized strings. Adopt Font.custom(_:size:relativeTo:) or @ScaledMetric and audit truncation at accessibility sizes.
acceptance_criteria: French mode contains no unintended English runtime states across map, sync, rewards, badges, errors, and loading; English remains correct; iOS layouts remain operable through accessibilityExtraExtraExtraLarge with no clipped primary actions; VoiceOver and TalkBack announce localized names and states.
close_reason: iOS now uses Dynamic Type-relative Inter fonts and localized state for GPS, missions, badges, and earned status; Android sync state is bilingual; iOS simulator build succeeds.

title: Remove forced portrait mode and restore user zoom
description: The web app blocks all interaction in landscape below 500px height while focus remains on hidden controls; index.html also sets maximum-scale=1 and user-scalable=no. Android MainActivity is locked to portrait. These fail WCAG 2.2 1.3.4 Orientation and 1.4.4 Resize Text and reproduce the reported mobile blocker.
design: Allow both orientations and responsive reflow. Remove the landscape-block gate and viewport zoom restrictions. Remove Android screenOrientation=portrait unless a narrowly documented essential exception exists. Preserve 48px field targets and map controls in short landscape layouts.
acceptance_criteria: Web remains fully operable at 844x390 and 390x844 with no blocking overlay; keyboard focus never lands behind an overlay; pinch zoom and at least 200% text scaling work; Android rotates without task loss; axe meta-viewport passes; device tests cover portrait and landscape.
close_reason: Removed zoom restriction, web landscape gate, native orientation lock, and Android portrait manifest lock; added responsive accessibility regression coverage.

title: Password reset flow (DIY token + transactional email)
description:
Why: agents have no recovery path; lost-phone or forgotten-password = lost data + lost trust. Auth uses @auth/core + bcrypt with users table; Supabase Auth not wired and not worth migrating for one feature.

How to apply: build a self-contained reset flow.

Scope:
- Schema: password_reset_tokens(token_hash, user_id, expires_at, used_at) with single-use enforcement.
- POST /api/auth/password-reset/request — accepts email/phone, generates token, dispatches email via the transactional pipeline (depends on email provider sub-issue). Always returns 200 to avoid user-enumeration.
- POST /api/auth/password-reset/confirm — verifies token, sets new bcrypt hash, invalidates session.
- UI: 'Forgot password' link on Auth.tsx → request form → confirmation screen. New ResetPassword.tsx screen for the link target.
- Rate limiting via lib/server/rateLimit.ts (per-IP and per-identifier).
- Tests: tests/passwordReset.test.ts covering happy path, expired token, reused token, invalid token, enumeration safety.

Acceptance:
- Token TTL 30 min, single-use, hashed at rest.
- Reset success invalidates other active sessions for that user.
- Bilingual EN/FR copy via t() pattern.
- No user-enumeration in API responses.

title: Admin review panel shows Approve/Reject buttons after decision already made
description:
When admin opens a submission already approved (or rejected), the review detail panel still renders the Approve and Reject action buttons at components/Screens/AdminQueue.tsx:1839, allowing redundant re-decisions and confusing the reviewer.

Why: Trust and forensic clarity are core (CLAUDE.md design principles). The admin role register is 'clinical, information-dense, forensic' — showing actionable controls for an already-finalized decision breaks that contract and risks accidental status flips via PATCH /api/submissions/:id?view=review.

Repro: 1) Sign in as admin. 2) Open AdminQueue. 3) Approve a pending submission. 4) Re-select same submission (or its group). 5) Approve/Reject buttons still render with no indication that decision is final.

Root cause: Button group renders unconditionally inside the selectedGroup detail panel; no check on submission.reviewDecision / selectedGroup.summary.reviewStatus. applyReviewToLocalState sets reviewStatus to 'auto_approved' on approve but UI ignores it for action visibility.

Acceptance:
- If latest event reviewStatus is approved/auto_approved/rejected_manual/rejected_out_of_zone, hide the Approve+Reject grid OR replace with disabled state + explicit 'Override decision' affordance.
- Show current decision badge (Approved/Rejected, by whom, when) in the panel.
- Keyboard shortcuts (A/R/H at line 717-730) must respect the same guard — no silent re-decision.
- Bilingual EN/FR strings via t() pattern.
- Test: tests/ adminReview test verifying API not called when buttons hidden.

title: Scale: DB rate-limiter writes Postgres on every request

close_reason: Resolved (decision, not migration): after reading call sites, all remaining consumeRateLimit (Postgres) users are LOW-FREQUENCY durable abuse/cost caps — hourly windows on auth login/register, password reset, /api/ai/* (paid), POST /api/submissions, privacy. Not a QPS hot path; Postgres persistence + logSecurityEvent audit are intended features (survive restarts, abuse trail). The genuine correctness win (atomic cross-instance bucket) shipped in 3d89a40. title: Scale: point-events read path materializes every row, no spatial/time filter

CAVEAT: global scope (admin-only) stays unbounded by design — no bbox; point-group pagination still JS-side over the now-bounded set (true DB-side point pagination deferred as separate epic).
close_reason: Code done + pushed on fix/bd-sex-read-path-bbox (7898beb). SQL bbox+since pushed to Postgres via buildPointEventsQuery; behavior-preserving; CI green. Migration 20260602_point_events_spatial_index applied alongside others under bd-xn8's migrate run.

title: Idempotency: consolidate two divergent implementations

close_reason: Done in b3f1aec: unified into idempotencyCore (one decision fn + one hash + one header alias set incl legacy x-idempotency-key). Both stores share semantics. NOTE: kept two tables (no risky data migration) per Fork B; full table merge deliberately deferred.

title: Idempotency: concurrent in-flight duplicates both proceed (no single-flight)
description: reserve/complete without row lock: two simultaneous same-key requests both get 'reserved' and both write. Needs SELECT FOR UPDATE or advisory lock protocol. Pre-existing; separate from the 500/orphan bugs.
close_reason: Fixed in b3f1aec: single-flight via insert-race winner detection. Only the caller whose INSERT returns rowCount=1 executes; concurrent losers re-read -> in_flight -> handler returns 409+Retry-After instead of double-executing. Applies to both stores via idempotencyCore.classifyExistingReservation.

title: Admin Communications dashboard (audience + composer + status)
description:
Why: admins need a single surface to compose, schedule, dry-run, and monitor campaigns across email + SMS + in-app. Lives inside AdminQueue as a new mode (consistent with assignments / review modes).

Scope:
- New activeMode='communications' in components/Screens/AdminQueue.tsx.
- Audience builder UI: role chips, trust tier multi-select, vertical scope, geofence picker, last-active window slider, opt-in toggle. Live recipient count.
- Composer: subject + body (rich text optional, plain text required), bilingual side-by-side EN/FR fields, channel toggles (email/SMS/in-app), variable insertion ({firstName}, {city}).
- Dry-run: render preview against 5 sampled recipients.
- Schedule: now / scheduled_at picker.
- Status board: list campaigns with sent/queued/failed/opened counts, drill into per-recipient log.
- Permissions: only role=admin; audit log entry per campaign creation.

Acceptance:
- Cannot send without confirming recipient count + estimated provider cost.
- Bilingual templates required for marketing-class campaigns; transactional may be single-language.
- Per-campaign pause/cancel from status board.

title: Mass SMS broadcaster (Cameroon-friendly provider, sender ID, opt-in)
description:
Why: many agents register with phone-only and respond faster to SMS than email. Cameroon-specific delivery requires either Africa's Talking (direct MTN/Orange peering, lower cost) or Twilio (broader DLR, higher cost).

Scope:
- Provider abstraction lib/server/sms/provider.ts with adapters africasTalking + twilio, selectable via SMS_PROVIDER env.
- Schema: users.smsOptIn boolean default false (opt-in, not opt-out — SMS stricter than email). sms_campaigns + sms_recipients mirroring email schema. STOP/UNSUBSCRIBE keyword handling via inbound webhook flips smsOptIn=false.
- Sender ID registration: document the Cameroon ARTP process, store sender ID per env, fall back to long code where unregistered.
- Cost guard: per-campaign max-spend threshold + admin confirm > .
- Truncate + segment counter in admin UI; warn on >1 segment.

Acceptance:
- STOP keyword unsubscribes within 1 message round-trip.
- Inbound webhook signature verified.
- Per-recipient retry capped (SMS retries cost real money).
- Audit log records sender ID + cost estimate per send.

title: Mass email broadcaster (audience filter + send queue + delivery tracking)
description:
Why: enable admins to send campaign + operational emails to filtered subsets of agents (incident comms, payout notices, region-specific announcements).

Scope:
- Audience builder: filter by role, trustTier, geofence (city/zone), language, last-active window, opt-in status.
- email_campaigns(id, subject, html_body, text_body, audience_query, scheduled_at, status, created_by). campaign_recipients(campaign_id, user_id, status, error). Both written transactionally on dispatch.
- Send worker: cron-drained batch (Vercel Cron every minute) draining pending recipients in chunks of N to respect Resend rate limits and 300s function timeout. Idempotent per recipient via unique key.
- Dry-run preview: render template + count audience without sending.
- Bilingual templates (EN/FR) per recipient.user.language.
- Suppression + unsubscribe enforced at fan-out time, not just dispatch time.

Acceptance:
- 10k-recipient campaign completes within 1h without timing out a function.
- Per-recipient retry on transient provider errors, dead-letter on permanent.
- Admin can pause/cancel a running campaign.
- communications_log links back to campaign_id for unified tracking.

title: Transactional + bulk email provider integration (Resend)
description:
Why: foundation for password reset, mass email, system notifications. Resend chosen for native Vercel Marketplace integration, audiences API, batch send, and webhook delivery events.

Scope:
- Provider client: lib/server/email/provider.ts wrapping Resend SDK with sendTransactional() and sendBatch().
- Schema additions to users: emailOptIn boolean default true, unsubscribeToken text unique. communications_log(user_id, channel, template_id, status, provider_message_id, sent_at, opened_at, clicked_at).
- Suppression list table + enforcement in send path.
- Unsubscribe endpoint GET /api/comms/unsubscribe?token=… (one-click, no auth).
- Webhook /api/comms/email/webhook ingesting Resend delivery + bounce + complaint events.
- Compliance: CAN-SPAM List-Unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe-Post headers, physical address footer, plain-text alt body.
- Env: RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_FROM, RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

Acceptance:
- sendTransactional rejects suppressed addresses pre-flight.
- Unsubscribe link always present in marketing-class emails; absent only in transactional (per Resend + CAN-SPAM).
- Webhook updates communications_log status reliably; idempotent on retry.

title: Epic: User communications platform — password reset, mass email, mass SMS, admin console
description:
Why: users currently have no way to recover lost passwords (auth/core + bcrypt, no reset flow), and the team has no channel for at-scale outreach to agents (incident comms, campaign launches, payout notices, policy updates). Cameroon pilot will need this before scaling beyond Bonamoussadi.

Scope:
1. Password reset (DIY token + transactional email).
2. Transactional email pipeline (provider integration + suppression + unsubscribe).
3. Mass email broadcaster (audience filter + send queue + delivery tracking).
4. Mass SMS broadcaster (Cameroon-friendly provider, sender ID, opt-in).
5. Admin Communications dashboard (mode in AdminQueue with audience builder, template editor, dry-run, schedule, status).

Why NOT Supabase Auth: project uses Supabase only as Postgres host via pg driver. Auth runs on @auth/core with custom users table + bcrypt hashes. Adopting Supabase Auth = full migration of sessions, OAuth, admin bootstrap, user_id FKs. Out of scope; revisit later as separate epic if ever justified.

Acceptance:
- Agent can request password reset via email/phone, receive secure single-use link/code, set new password.
- Admin can compose + send a campaign to a filtered audience (role, trust tier, geofence, last-active) across email or SMS.
- Every recipient has unsubscribe + opt-in tracked. Suppression list enforced server-side.
- Send jobs survive function timeouts (queue + worker model, not synchronous fan-out).
- Compliance: CAN-SPAM headers, GDPR lawful basis recorded, Cameroon ARTP SMS sender ID registered.

Sub-issues to follow.

title: API tests: platform_enrich_gps_required 422 + gate-order short-circuit

Fine at pilot scale, will not scale multi-city. Add bound/cache/filtered query before scale-out.

title: Enrich idempotent-replay returns 429 instead of 201
description: Cooldown gate in handleRecordCreate runs before the ON-CONFLICT idempotency path (lib/server/platform/api.ts). A same-key retry of a successful enrich submission (dropped response) hits hasRecentRecordForPoint=true and gets 429 platform_enrich_cooldown instead of the idempotent 201. Record is saved either way — misleading message, not data loss. Fix: short-circuit cooldown when the same idempotency key already produced a record, or check idempotency before the gates. Found by final whole-branch review of feature/platform-point-enrichment.

title: Polish review-queue visual semantics
description: The Impeccable detector found three recurring anti-patterns: a decorative left-border side-tab treatment and gray text on red status surfaces in AdminQueue and SubmissionQueue. These weaken status semantics and consistency.
design: Replace decorative side-border emphasis with the shared selected-state pattern and use semantic danger text tokens on danger washes. Re-run the detector and contrast checks.
acceptance_criteria: Impeccable detector reports no side-tab or gray-on-color findings in review queues; statuses use semantic shared tokens; visual hierarchy remains clear at mobile and desktop sizes.
close_reason: Removed decorative side-tab border and gray-on-danger combinations; queue statuses now use semantic full borders and danger contrast tokens.

title: Add native AppReleaseMode regression coverage
description: Add explicit native iOS test coverage for DEBUG vs Release AppReleaseMode behavior once the Xcode test target/tooling is available, covering normalizedRole, defaultTab, tabs, and demo/role-switching flags.
acceptance_criteria: Tests assert Release normalizes admin/client to agent tabs only; Debug keeps all demo roles and non-agent tabs; tests run in Xcode or documented native test command.

title: Lock native category during selected-point enrichment
description: When the native iOS map detail sheet starts capture for an existing point, the capture form emits ENRICH_EVENT for that pointId but still allows changing the category picker. Lock or derive category from the selected point during enrichment to avoid mismatched point/category submissions.
acceptance_criteria: Selected-point enrichment preserves the selected point category; category picker is disabled or hidden for enrichment mode; CREATE_EVENT map-center capture still allows category selection; Swift parse/static checks pass.

title: Scale: in-memory bucket store is per-instance under Fluid Compute
description: createInMemoryBucketStore (store.ts:11) is per-instance heap; effective burst limit = instances x capacity when Upstash unset (rateLimit.ts:133). Document as best-effort; require Upstash in prod.
close_reason: Fixed in 3d89a40: added atomic BucketStore.consumeTokenBucket (in-memory sync/yield-free; Redis fixed Lua eval, atomic cross-instance) — fixes the get/set read-modify-write race. resolveBucketStore now logs a loud prod warning when Upstash unset (per-instance best-effort, effective limit = instances x capacity). Upstash required in prod (see bd-xn8).

title: Email broadcast runbook documented

close_reason: Closed
